title: GitHub fine-grained PAT spec
description: Declarative spec consumed by scripts/form.mjs to drive GitHub's fine-grained personal-access-token creation UI. There is NO API to create fine-grained PATs (web UI only), so this spec is the machine-readable SSoT the browser engine reads.

name.description: Token name. Must be unique on the account.

owner.description: Resource-owner login. Omit to use the authenticated user. Set to an org login for org-owned tokens.

expiration.description: Token lifetime: 7/30/60/90 (days), an ISO date 'YYYY-MM-DD' (custom), or 'none' (no expiration — triggers a confirm modal).

repositoryAccess.description: Which repositories the token can touch. 'public' = read-only public repos (use for account-only tokens); 'all' = every repo the owner has; 'selected' = the explicit 'repos' list.

permissions.description: UI permission rows to set. Key = exact permission label as shown in GitHub's 'Add permissions' menu (e.g. 'Contents', 'Pull requests', 'Commit statuses', 'Gists'). Value: 'read' -> 'Read-only', 'write' -> 'Read and write'. 'Metadata' is auto-required read-only and must NOT be listed.
